title: Good Friday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 20, 2019 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

April 20, 2019 was Good Friday in the Christian calendar, the holiest day of the Episcopal liturgical year, falling within Holy Week and preceding Easter Sunday (April 21). The readings for Good Friday follow the traditional three-hour liturgy, featuring Isaiah 52:13-53:12 (the Suffering Servant), Psalm 22, Hebrews 10:16-25, and the Passion Gospel account (typically John 18:1-19:37 in the Book of Common Prayer). This day falls in the season of Lent, specifically at its culmination, marking the commemoration of Christ's crucifixion.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of the 2018 General Convention (held in Austin, Texas in July 2018), which had approved allowing any baptized person to receive communion and had reaffirmed the church's openness to LGBTQ+ inclusion. Bishop Robert Wright of the Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral) had been serving since 2011 and was navigating ongoing discussions about inclusion and doctrine within both the national church and the Rocky Mountain region. No major diocesan elections or crises were immediately pending in April 2019.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in mid-April 2019 was experiencing typical spring weather patterns, with temperatures fluctuating in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit. The city was focused on municipal governance following the 2019 mayoral election cycle (Mayor Michael Hancock was serving his second term). The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was experiencing continued gentrification and development pressures that had been ongoing for several years. No major weather events, natural disasters, or immediate local crises affected Denver specifically in the week before April 20.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Mueller Report on Russian interference in the 2016 election had been released on April 18, 2019 (two days before this sermon), dominating national news with debates over obstruction of justice findings and Attorney General William Barr's summary. The 2020 presidential election cycle was intensifying, with multiple Democratic candidates campaigning actively. The Trump administration was engaged in escalating trade tensions with China, and immigration remained a contentious political issue at the southern border.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Sri Lanka was reeling from the Easter Sunday bombing attacks that had occurred just one day earlier, on April 21, 2019 (note: this was actually Easter Sunday itself, April 21, so the attacks occurred the day after this sermon), which killed over 250 people and injured hundreds more. The attacks on Catholic churches and luxury hotels were attributed to local extremist groups with alleged international connections. The Syrian conflict continued with ongoing humanitarian crisis and international diplomatic tensions over chemical weapons use.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in April 2019 was dominated by the conclusion of the Marvel Cinematic Universe's "Avengers: Endgame" storyline (the film was released April 26, 2019, one week later), with intense cultural anticipation. The college basketball season had just concluded with March Madness, and the 2019 NFL Draft was approaching. The zeitgeist reflected deep political polarization following the Mueller Report release, with Americans divided along partisan lines about its implications. For many Christian congregations, Holy Week represents a moment of spiritual intensity and countercultural reflection amid secular cultural noise.
